ucs.title: [LLM] LAPSUS$ MFA fatigue / push-bombing followed by privileged role assignment (Strawberry Tempest)
ucs.description: Hunts LAPSUS$'s signature initial-access chain: harvest creds from stealer logs / underground markets, then bombard the user with Azure AD MFA push prompts until one is approved, immediately followed by sign-in from a residential/VPN proxy and self-elevation via AAD role or group membership change. This is the exact playbook MSTIC documented for DEV-0537 against Okta, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Uber.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Authentication.src) as src values(Authentication.app) as app from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=failure Authentication.signature_id IN ("50074","500121","50097","50158","500021") OR Authentication.reason="MFA denied" by Authentication.user _time span=10m
| eventstats dc(_time) as denied_buckets sum(count) as denied_pushes by Authentication.user
| where denied_pushes >= 5
| join type=inner Authentication.user [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Authentication.src) as success_src values(Authentication.app) as success_app min(_time) as mfa_success_time from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.signature_id IN ("0","50140") by Authentication.user
    | rename Authentication.user as Authentication.user ]
| where mfa_success_time >= firstTime AND mfa_success_time <= (lastTime + 1800)
| join type=inner Authentication.user [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Change where Change.action=modified Change.object_category IN ("user","group","role") (Change.change_type="AAD" OR Change.vendor_product="Azure AD") by Change.user Change.object Change.command _time
    | rename Change.user as Authentication.user ]
| table firstTime lastTime Authentication.user denied_pushes success_src success_app Change.object Change.command
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let window = 30m;
let pushBombing = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ErrorCode in (50074, 500121, 50097, 50158, 500021) // MFA denied / not satisfied / user declined
| summarize DeniedPushes = count(), FirstDeny = min(Timestamp), LastDeny = max(Timestamp), DenySrcIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 25) by AccountUpn, AccountObjectId
| where DeniedPushes >= 5;
let successAfterBomb = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ErrorCode == 0
| project SuccessTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, AccountObjectId, SuccessIP = IPAddress, Country, ISP = NetworkLocationDetails, App = Application, DeviceTrust = DeviceTrustType;
pushBombing
| join kind=inner successAfterBomb on AccountObjectId
| where SuccessTime between (FirstDeny .. (LastDeny + window))
| join kind=inner (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where ActionType in ("Add member to role.", "Add eligible member to role.", "Add member to group.", "Update user.", "Add owner to application.", "Consent to application.")
    | extend TargetUpn = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).ObjectId)
    | project ElevationTime = Timestamp, ActorUpn = AccountDisplayName, ActionType, TargetUpn, RawEventData
) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.ActorUpn
| where ElevationTime between (SuccessTime .. (SuccessTime + 2h))
| project FirstDeny, LastDeny, DeniedPushes, AccountUpn, SuccessTime, SuccessIP, Country, ISP, App, ElevationTime, ActionType, TargetUpn
ucs.rationale: LAPSUS$ does not deploy ransomware or custom malware - their entire intrusion economy is bought creds + MFA push fatigue + cloud-role abuse. The pattern of >=5 denied pushes followed within 30 min by a successful sign-in AND an AAD role/group change within 2 hours is the exact tradecraft MSTIC published for DEV-0537 (NVIDIA, Microsoft source-code theft, Okta sub-processor). Generic MFA-spam rules

ucs.title: [LLM] LAPSUS$ insider-recruitment & SharePoint/Confluence source-code exfil (DEV-0537 collection stage)
ucs.description: After credential access, LAPSUS$ pivots to collaboration tooling (SharePoint Online, Confluence, GitLab, Jira) to mass-download source code, secrets, and credentials before posting them to their Telegram channel. Hunts abnormal high-volume reads from these repositories by an account that just had a risky/foreign sign-in - characteristic of the Samsung, Microsoft Bing/Cortana, and Vodafone source-leak intrusions.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.user_agent) as ua sum(Web.bytes_out) as bytes_out sum(Web.bytes_in) as bytes_in from datamodel=Web where (Web.url IN ("*sharepoint.com*","*_api/web/*","*/_layouts/15/download.aspx*","*atlassian.net/wiki/rest/api/content*","*gitlab*","*/api/v4/projects/*/repository/archive*","*bitbucket*")) by Web.user Web.src _time span=15m
| where bytes_in > 524288000 OR mvcount(urls) > 500
| join type=inner Web.user [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Authentication.src) as auth_src values(Authentication.signature) as risk from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success (Authentication.signature="*risky*" OR Authentication.app="Azure Active Directory") by Authentication.user
    | rename Authentication.user as Web.user ]
| eval risk_indicators=if(match(auth_src,"^(?:5\.188\.|45\.146\.|185\.220\.|198\.98\.|tor)"), "anon-proxy", "normal")
| where risk_indicators="anon-proxy" OR like(risk,"%risky%")
| table _time Web.user Web.src auth_src bytes_in mvcount(urls) urls
| sort -bytes_in
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 1d;
let riskyUsers = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("high","medium") or RiskState == "atRisk" or isnotempty(RiskEventTypes)
| summarize RiskyIPs = make_set(IPAddress,10), RiskyCountries = make_set(Country,5), FirstRisk = min(Timestamp) by AccountUpn, AccountObjectId;
let repoBurst = CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where Application in ("Microsoft SharePoint Online","Microsoft OneDrive for Business","GitHub","GitLab","Atlassian","Confluence","Bitbucket")
| where ActionType in ("FileDownloaded","FileSyncDownloadedFull","FileAccessed","PageViewed","SearchQueryPerformed","Repo.Clone","Repo.Download","PullRequest.Read")
| summarize Downloads = count(),
            DistinctFiles = dcount(ObjectName),
            Sites = make_set(tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).SiteUrl), 20),
            Apps = make_set(Application, 10),
            FirstAct = min(Timestamp), LastAct = max(Timestamp),
            IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 10)
  by AccountObjectId, AccountDisplayName
| where DistinctFiles > 200 or Downloads > 500;
riskyUsers
| join kind=inner repoBurst on AccountObjectId
| where FirstAct between (FirstRisk .. (FirstRisk + 6h))
| project AccountDisplayName, AccountUpn, RiskyIPs, RiskyCountries, FirstRisk, FirstAct, LastAct, Downloads, DistinctFiles, Apps, Sites, IPs
| order by DistinctFiles desc
ucs.rationale: LAPSUS$ is a collection-and-leak crew, not an encryptor. Microsoft's DEV-0537 report and the Samsung/NVIDIA/Microsoft/Vodafone leaks all show the same pattern: compromised cloud account → SharePoint/Confluence/GitLab mass-pull → Telegram dump. Pairing the cloud-repo download burst with a preceding risky sign-in (the bought-credential or MFA-bombed session) ties the alert to LAPSUS$ TTPs rather tha
